Instructional_Objectives: Explain the need of cyber security
List out the major security problems
Differentiate between cyber crime, cyber attack and cyber terrorism
Entry_Behaviour: Do you have social media accounts?
Have you ever heard that any content of your friends' account is hacked?
Instructional_Aids: Illustrative Board representing major security problems

Content_Sections.Learning_Experience: Any offence committed against reputation or cause physical or mental trauma through electronic means can be defined as cyber crime. Electronic means include internet, chat rooms, emails, mobile phones, Bluetooth, SMS.
Content_Sections.Evaluation: What is cyber crime? Does cyber crime include electronic means?

Content_Sections.Learning_Experience: Cyber security is important because it protects all categories of data from theft and damage. It helps keep private data like passwords and photos safe from hackers and prevents loss of money due to online scams.
Content_Sections.Behavioral_Objectives: Explain the need of cyber security
Content_Sections.Evaluation: Explain the need of cyber security.

Content_Sections.Learning_Experience: 1. Variety of Devices: Different devices connect to the internet, not all have strong security. 2. Lack of common standards: Difficult to set same security rules for every country. 3. Poor Data Protection: Weak passwords make it easier for attackers. 4. Low Digital Awareness: Users don't know about safe practices.
Content_Sections.Behavioral_Objectives: List out the major security problems
Content_Sections.Evaluation: How lack of common standards and poor data protection lead to security problems?

Content_Sections.Content: Cyber Crime vs Attack vs Terrorism
Content_Sections.Learning_Experience: Cyber Crime: Criminal activity involving a computer. Cyber Attack: Stealing and destroying data. Cyber Terrorism: Using technologies to cause violence/panic.
Content_Sections.Behavioral_Objectives: Differentiate between cyber crime, cyber attack and cyber terrorism
Content_Sections.Evaluation: What is the difference between cyber crime and cyber attack?

Instructional_Objectives: Enlist the types of cyber crime
Explain the types of cyber crime
Give example of types of cyber crime

Content_Sections.Learning_Experience: Adept and advanced hackers who attack the computer are termed as cyber criminals.
Content_Sections.Evaluation: What do you mean by cyber criminals?

Content_Sections.Learning_Experience: Malware is a form of application that performs malicious activities. Example: Ransomware (2017) designed to encrypt files and ask for ransom.
Hackers trick a target into handing over valuable info like passwords or credit card details. Example: Message regarding OTP with a clickable link.
Interception of message between two parties to spy or steal data. Example: Fake looking public WIFI hotspot.
Hacker floods a target server with traffic to disrupt it. Example: GitHub DDoS (2018).
Content_Sections.Evaluation: What is the full form of DDoS?

Content_Sections.Learning_Experience: Targeting a computer to mine cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin.
Theft of personal information of an individual to avail financial services.
Content_Sections.Evaluation: What is meant by identity theft?

Content_Sections.Learning_Experience: Gaining unauthorized access to a computer system or network by exploiting vulnerabilities.
Cyber crime crossing national borders involving actions of at least one nation state.

Instructional_Objectives: Enlist common signs of computer virus

Content_Sections.Learning_Experience: A malicious computer program which when executed replicates itself and inserts its own code. First virus 'Creeper' (1971) by Bob Thomas.
Content_Sections.Evaluation: What is computer virus? Name two viruses.

Content_Sections.Learning_Experience: System running slower, pop-up windows, crashing of device, mass emails sent from account, changes to homepage.
Content_Sections.Evaluation: Enlist common signs. How can a device be crashed?

Instructional_Objectives: Differentiate between computer virus and worm

Content_Sections.Learning_Experience: Self-replicating malicious software that spreads automatically through networks without needing to attach to a file.
Email worms (I Love You), File-sharing worms (Klez), Internet worms (Nimda), Instant Messaging worms.
Content_Sections.Evaluation: How does an email worm spread?

Content_Sections.Learning_Experience: Virus needs human action and a host file. Worm is self-replicating and spreads automatically.
Content_Sections.Evaluation: Why does a virus need a host whereas a worm does not?
